channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: A quality model for machine learning components
item.description: This post presents a quality model for machine learning components that serves as a guide for requirements elicitation and negotiation.

item.title: An ipsc-derived neural progenitor cell therapy for subacute spinal cord injury: a phase 1 trial with long-term follow-up
item.description: A phase 1 trial evaluating the safety of iPSC-derived neural stem cell transplantation in subacute complete spinal cord injury, with 2–4 years of follow-up including MRI, neurological and functional assessments met its primary outcome.

item.title: Automotive giants test repsol renewable gasoline
item.description: BMW and Toyota began a six-month pilot deployment of Repsol's Nexa 95 renewable gasoline to test the scalability of renewable fuel on existing vehicles.

item.title: Anthropic's landmark $1.5b copyright settlement is approved | techcrunch
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: Adaptive nanocarriers targeting tumor systemic intelligence: A paradigm to overcome systemic barriers in precision drug delivery
item.description: Adaptive nanocarriers represent a novel approach to precision drug delivery that can overcome systemic barriers by intelligently targeting tumor microenvironments. This paradigm leverages nanocarrier technology to interact with tumor-associated systemic factors, potentially improving therapeutic eff

item.title: STAT+: Anthropic deepens work with rare disease drugs
item.description: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1s, Medicare pricing plans, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: Exosomes in cancer drug resistance: dual roles in therapy failure and emerging precision therapeutics
item.description: This review examines the dual roles of exosomes in cancer drug resistance, exploring how these extracellular vesicles contribute to therapy failure while simultaneously presenting opportunities for precision therapeutic development. The article discusses mechanistic pathways through which exosomes m

item.title: STAT+: Telemedicine company touted by Novo Nordisk stressed profits over patient safety, ex-workers say
item.description: LifeMD, a popular telehealth company, has been accused by former employees of pressing clinicians to mete out prescriptions more rapidly without sufficient screening and follow-up.

item.title: Before q-day: the race to quantum first
item.description: Somewhere, a hard drive is filling up with secrets no one can read yet, and its owner is waiting for the machine that opens them all at once. On March 30, 2026, that wait got shorter.Two independent research teams lowered the public estimates for breaking the…

item.title: Nature needs mathematics for its rigorous description but is computation at the root of nature
item.description: Feynman’s made a lecture and book that mathematics is needed to describe nature. It (in The Relation of Mathematics to Physics) is descriptive and he is slightly awestruck. The fundamental laws turn out to be statable in mathematics, and there’s no obvious re…

item.title: AI transparency: governance, explainability, and data practices
item.description: * AI transparency documents an AI system's data, model behavior, and decision-making processes, and is distinct from explainability and interpretability, which address narrower questions about individual predictions and internal model logic. * Regulatory pre…

item.title: Stop writing user stories for AI agents: they need decision contracts
item.description: User stories can describe what an AI agent should accomplish—but they rarely define how much authority it should have, when it must stop, or who is accountable when it gets a decision wrong. This article introduces the AI Decision Contract, a practical Busine…

item.title: Extending genome-wide association studies to admixed cohorts with high degrees of relatedness
item.description: Tractor-Mix is a GWAS method for admixed cohorts with high relatedness, improving ancestry-specific effect estimation, controlling false positives and boosting power to detect ancestry-enriched loci across diverse populations.

item.title: What should the US and its allies treat as “critical”?
item.description: Economic dependencies can turn into weapons. Here’s how policymakers can spot true vulnerabilities and defend themselves.

item.title: AI ops tools will create console sprawl and break it more often: gartner
item.description: Yet by 2030, a quarter of the IT ops work you do now will be handled by an unsupervised clanker

item.title: Ultra-low temperature storage of advanced therapy medicinal products in hospital pharmacies: findings from a national survey of French hospital pharmacists
item.description: A national survey of French hospital pharmacists examined current practices and infrastructure for ultra-low temperature storage of advanced therapy medicinal products in hospital pharmacy settings. The study provides empirical findings on how French hospitals manage the specialized storage requirem

item.title: Phase III LUNA clinical programme: design of four randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies assessing the efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in patients with symptomatic COPD and a high risk of exacerbations
item.description: The Phase III LUNA clinical programme comprises four randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of tozorakimab in patients with symptomatic COPD who are at high risk of exacerbations. This multi-study approach aims to generate comprehensive evid

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

item.title: Exosomal gene-based predictive model and therapeutic target identification for alzheimer’s disease: a bioinformatics analysis
item.description: Background Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a degenerative central nervous system disorder characterized by progressive cognitive and behavioral impairment. As nanoscale intercellular communication vesicles that carry AD-related pathological molecules, exosomes ar…

item.title: The ethics of artificial intelligence in scientific discovery
item.description: The Ethics of Artificial Intelligence in Scientific Discovery Artificial Intelligence (AI) has become a powerful force in transforming the landscape of scientific discovery. An AI approach to problem solving is required in practically every field of study, be…

item.title: UK biotech financing continues recovery trajectory on five-year q2 high
item.description: While UK biotech financing recovery is present in the private markets, BIA CEO Chris Molloy notes that UK public sector backing is essential for sustained gr...

item.title: Bristol myers buys nvidia's latest AI computing system for drug research
item.description: NEW YORK, July 20 : Bristol Myers Squibb said on Monday it is buying the latest-generation computing system from chip company Nvidia to support its use of artificial intelligence across its drug discovery and development operations.The drugmaker said it will …

item.title: Daily briefing: how to nail an industry interview
item.description: Careers coaches shed some light on multi-stage interviews for roles in industry. Plus, a man’s ability to make sperm has been restored by a testicular-tissue transplant and the deadly toll of a ‘disease of the built environment’.

  • Meg…

item.title: Weekly climate and energy news roundup #698
item.description: “The only thing more dangerous than ignorance is arrogance.” — Albert Einstein The post Weekly Climate and Energy News Roundup #698 appeared first on Watts Up With That?.

item.title: Agentic commerce: why AI agents are transforming the ecommerce landscape
item.description: How AI agents are revolutionizing payments and ushering in the era of agentic commerce.

item.title: Preventing deskilling is the next step in india’s health transformation - express healthcare
item.description: Santanu Mishra, Co-founder, Smile Foundation argues that while AI can enhance efficiency and improve access, maintaining human vigilance and preventing the de-skilling of medical trainees is a profound sociological challenge and very important to the medical …

item.title: How primary care can implement patient-centered AI policy
item.description: Patients are aware of the risks of AI use, so primary care offices need to implement a patient-centered AI policy enabling disclosure and consent.

item.title: Aws' allyson fryhoff on how health systems are using AI to analyze complex data
item.description: In a Q&A, AWS' managing director of healthcare and life sciences, Allyson Fryhoff, discussed how providers are using agentic AI to study population health and improve their workflows.

item.title: Multi-class, unsupervised detection and classification of biological and anthropogenic sounds in coral reefs
item.description: Author summary Detection and classification of biological sounds is essential for monitoring the restoration progress of coral reefs, yet most artificial intelligence (AI) methods require large, hand‑labeled training datasets, which are difficult to obtain in…

item.title: Tempus AI bets on mrd testing growth with personalis acquisition
item.description: Tempus AI (NASDAQ:TEM) executives said the company’s planned acquisition of Personalis is intended to strengthen its position in minimal residual disease, or...

item.title: A feature-efficient dual-task machine learning framework for predicting bone mineral density and osteoporosis stratification in resource-constrained environments
item.description: Osteoporosis is a chronic skeletal disorder characterized by progressive bone mineral density (BMD) loss and structural deterioration, significantly increasing fracture risk. Despite its high prevalence, early detection remains challenging due to its asymptom…

item.title: China’s new AI club: the world artificial intelligence cooperation organization
item.description: Global tech governance is taking an institutional turn as a bloc-based system continues to solidify.

item.title: What canadian wildfire smoke is actually doing to human bodies across north america
item.description: Canadian wildfire smoke is triggering asthma attacks, ER visits for heart attacks, and tens of thousands of premature deaths annually. Doctors and peer-reviewed research explain exactly how PM2.5 attacks the body — and who faces the greatest danger right now.

item.title: Hpv vaccination intention and its association with vaccine hesitancy and health beliefs among unvaccinated chinese medical college students: a cross-sectional study
item.description: Background Human papillomavirus (HPV) infection poses a significant public health challenge, and HPV vaccination uptake among Chinese college students remains suboptimal. Therefore, it is essential to conduct ongoing, in-depth investigations into the psycholo…

item.title: Academic and psychological determinants of mental stress among medical students in dhaka: a multinomial logistic regression approach
item.description: As stress among medical students is a global health concern due to comprehensive academic engagements, this study aimed to assess the educational and psychological factors related to mental stress among medical students in Dhaka city. A descriptive type of cr…

item.title: Nigeria leads africa’s telecom agenda ahead of itu conference
item.description: Nigeria on Monday assumed the chairmanship of the 7th Ordinary Session of the African Telecommunications Union (ATU) Conference Preparatory Committee (CPC), leading Africa's drive for a common position ahead of the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) …

item.title: Trump's latest AI czar has already resigned | techcrunch
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make gemini more efficient | techcrunch
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: Spacex in your index fund, explained
item.description: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble, and, in my opinion, terribly overpri…

item.title: Openai is scared of open-weight models. should the US be? | techcrunch
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

item.title: Malicious cloud customers can bring down the power grid
item.description: Datacenters tax utilities normally, so just imagine what they could do if workloads were designed to destroy

item.title: Frontier llms couldn't help hugging face fight off evil agents
item.description: Chinese open-weight model GLM 5.2 happily obliged

item.title: China’s AI models have trump’s AI world at war with itself
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the coun…

item.title: Dr. jill lepore on why AI backlash is vital for the future
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Harvard professor and New Yorker staff writer Dr. Jill Lepore about her new book, The Rise and Fall of the Artificial State, which comes out on August 25. Jill is one of the best writers there is at identifying institutional patterns i…
